steps.title: Find the key signal in the finding
steps.content: The researchers found that bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with waterbird community integrity than riprap. In plain terms: bulkheads damage bird communities more than riprap does. To illustrate this, you need a direct comparison showing that when bulkheads dominate, IWCI scores drop sharply, but when riprap dominates, scores stay higher.

steps.title: Match the comparison to the finding
steps.content:
Choice B compares Old Road (a high-bulkhead site with lower IWCI scores) to Miles (a high-riprap site with higher IWCI scores). This directly shows bulkheads correlating more negatively with bird integrity than riprap. Choice A compares two bulkhead-heavy sites to each other, sidestepping the bulkhead-vs.-riprap contrast. Choice C mixes both structure types at each site, muddying which structure causes the damage. Choice D leaves the percentages at Miles unspecified, failing to set up a clean comparison.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
